Title: Discovery and engineering of colchicine alkaloid biosynthesis

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal tropolone alkaloid precursor in colchicine biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Aromatic L-amino acid decarboxylase / TyDC-DDC, PLP-dependent decarboxylase
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in leaves; pathway engineering from phenylalanine and tyrosine
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in N. benthamiana with BvCYP76AD5; LC-MS/HILIC metabolite profiling showing dopamine production; pathway dropout analysis
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful module for supplying dopamine precursor when reconstructing colchicine alkaloid biosynthesis in heterologous hosts
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly co-expressed with other colchicine biosynthetic genes in G. superba transcriptome
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GsTyDC/DDC was highly co-expressed with colchicine pathway genes in G. superba. 2. Co-expression with BvCYP76AD5 in N. benthamiana produced dopamine. 3. Dopamine supply contributed significantly to engineered N-formyldemecolcine production.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected function upon transient leaf expression
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Module 2 engineering gene; supports precursor supply rather than late colchicine scaffold tailoring
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: G. superba rhizomes; colchicine alkaloids also enriched in seeds/corms in related plants
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes dopamine formation and thereby supports de novo production of colchicine alkaloids in engineered N. benthamiana

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal tropolone alkaloid precursor in colchicine biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated pathway engineering in leaves
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression as part of module 1; LC-MS detection of 4-HDCA; pathway engineering and dropout analysis for N-formyldemecolcine production
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering 4-HDCA supply toward colchicine alkaloid biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-clustered with colchicine biosynthetic genes in G. superba transcriptome
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GsAER was identified as a co-clustered putative alkenal reductase. 2. Inclusion in module 1 enabled 4-HDCA production in N. benthamiana. 3. Inclusion improved engineered N-formyldemecolcine yield.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected function upon transient leaf expression
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Module 1 precursor-supply gene; part of non-native engineering strategy rather than fully proven native route
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports precursor 4-HDCA formation and increases engineered colchicine alkaloid output

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal tropolone alkaloid precursor in colchicine biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Norcoclaurine synthase / plant Pictet-Spenglerase, PR10/Bet v 1-like fold
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression with module 1 and module 2; LC-MS/MS comparison to authentic compound 1; pathway completion to N-formyldemecolcine
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key enabling heterologous enzyme for synthetic entry into colchicine pathway from endogenous amino acid-derived precursors
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Δ24-CjNCS enabled Pictet-Spengler condensation of 4-HDCA and dopamine to form precursor 1 in N. benthamiana. 2. This bypassed the unidentified native colchicine Pictet-Spenglerase. 3. The product entry point enabled full pathway reconstruction to N-formyldemecolcine.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected function upon transient leaf expression
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Heterologous engineering substitute, not a native colchicine-pathway gene; chosen because NCS accepts aldehyde substrates similar to 4-HDCA
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Creates pathway entry metabolite 1 and enables de novo engineered colchicine alkaloid biosynthesis

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal tropolone alkaloid precursor in colchicine biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression with GsTyDC/DDC; HILIC LC-MS detection of L-DOPA/dopamine; pathway engineering to N-formyldemecolcine
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful heterologous hydroxylase to supply dopamine for synthetic colchicine alkaloid production
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BvCYP76AD5 was used as an engineering substitute for an unidentified native 3'-hydroxylase. 2. Co-expression with GsTyDC/DDC produced dopamine in N. benthamiana. 3. This precursor module supported full pathway production of N-formyldemecolcine.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected function upon transient leaf expression
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Non-native auxiliary engineering gene from betalain biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes dopamine supply for de novo colchicine alkaloid pathway reconstruction

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal precursor of colchicine with tropolone pharmacophore
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group from SAM to a ring C' hydroxyl of precursor 1 to form 2
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms early pathway intermediate en route to target product
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression and substrate co-infiltration in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro enzyme assay with crude lysate; transient in planta activity assay; LC-MS/MS metabolite identification; pathway dropout analysis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed and strongly co-expressed with GsNMT and multiple CYP candidates; enriched with colchicine-associated transcript cluster
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GsOMT1 methylates precursor 1 to produce intermediate 2. 2. In planta and in vitro assays confirmed activity. 3. GsOMT1 likely initiates the major route of early methylation in pathway reconstruction.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of intermediate 2 and supports downstream N-formyldemecolcine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential early tailoring enzyme for reconstructing colchicine alkaloid biosynthesis in heterologous plant hosts
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Dropout studies indicate N-methylation can occasionally precede O-methylation, but GsOMT1-first is likely major native route

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal precursor of colchicine with tropolone pharmacophore
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group from SAM to the amine of intermediate 2 to form 3
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms early pathway intermediate en route to target product
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in N. benthamiana; N-terminal truncation improved flux
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression; LC-MS/MS metabolite identification; comparison of full-length and truncated enzyme; pathway dropout analysis
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Predicted mitochondria for full-length protein due to N-terminal targeting peptide
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly correlated with GsOMT1 expression (Pearson r > 0.99)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GsNMT converts 2 to N-methylated intermediate 3. 2. A predicted mitochondrial transit peptide limited heterologous flux. 3. GsNMT can also act before GsOMT1, but this is likely a minor route.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of intermediate 3 and supports downstream colchicine alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required methyltransferase for pathway reconstruction; truncated versions improve heterologous productivity
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Full-length enzyme shows lower product yield in heterologous host due to targeting sequence
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GsNMTt was used in subsequent pathway engineering because truncation increased yield ~10-fold

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal precursor of colchicine with tropolone pharmacophore
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SAM-dependent N-methyltransferase lacking predicted N-terminal mitochondrial targeting sequence
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes N-methylation of intermediate 2 to form 3 with improved heterologous flux after transit peptide removal
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms early pathway intermediate en route to target product
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression and quantitative comparison to full-length GsNMT; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Retargeted away from predicted mitochondria by N-terminal truncation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly functional in transient leaf expression
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Removal of the predicted N-terminal targeting sequence increased downstream product formation about tenfold. 2. GsNMTt was therefore used for later pathway reconstruction. 3. The truncation improved engineered flux to N-formyldemecolcine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Greatly increases heterologous pathway yield compared with full-length GsNMT
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Preferred engineered version for heterologous colchicine alkaloid production
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Non-native engineered version; may not reflect native localization
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Engineering derivative rather than distinct native gene

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal precursor of colchicine with tropolone pharmacophore
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Compound 4; likely contributes to compound 6 formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates ring A meta position of intermediate 3 to form 4; likely also hydroxylates 5 to form 6
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms intermediate and helps prepare substrate for subsequent methylations
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient co-expression in N. benthamiana; improved flux observed with GsNMT truncation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression; LC-MS/MS structural assignment; pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GsCYP75A109 converts 3 to hydroxylated intermediate 4. 2. Data suggest it may also hydroxylate downstream methylated intermediate 5 to give 6. 3. This enzyme is required for progression toward autumnaline and N-formyldemecolcine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Introduces critical hydroxylation(s) needed for progression through the colchicine pathway
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential oxidative tailoring enzyme for heterologous pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Exact sequence of hydroxylation versus methylation around compounds 4-6 is partly inferential; endogenous N. benthamiana hydroxylase contribution cannot be fully excluded for one step
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Catalytic assignment for formation of 6 is plausible but one alternative involves endogenous host activity

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal precursor of colchicine with tropolone pharmacophore
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Likely compound 5 (not directly observed); observed downstream product compound 6 in presence of hydroxylase activity
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely methylates the meta hydroxyl on ring A of compound 4 to form compound 5, which is rapidly converted to 6
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor for downstream autumnaline formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression; LC-MS/MS of observed product 6; pathway logic and isotope-labeling consistency
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GsOMT2 consumes 4 and leads to formation of 6. 2. The most likely role is methylation of 4 to produce known precursor 5, followed by hydroxylation to 6. 3. This step advances pathway flux toward autumnaline.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes conversion of hydroxylated intermediate toward downstream autumnaline branch
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required methyltransferase for mid-pathway reconstruction, though product capture may depend on coupled hydroxylation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Immediate product 5 was not directly observed; host hydroxylase contribution cannot be fully excluded
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Catalytic role inferred from pathway logic and observed mass changes rather than direct isolation of 5

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal precursor of colchicine with tropolone pharmacophore
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms confirmed downstream intermediate en route to target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression; LC-MS/MS; comparison to racemic autumnaline standard
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GsOMT3 converts 6 to autumnaline (7). 2. Product identity was confirmed by comparison to standard. 3. Access to a known natural intermediate validated the reconstituted pathway direction.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes production of autumnaline, a verified colchicine precursor
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Necessary methyltransferase for advancing engineered pathway into validated natural intermediate space
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Absolute stereochemistry of heterologously produced 7 was inferred, not directly resolved
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Likely generates (S)-autumnaline in context of pathway engineering

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal precursor of colchicine with tropolone pharmacophore
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Isoandrocymbine (8) isomeric peak set, one of which feeds downstream
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidative para-para phenol coupling of autumnaline to form isoandrocymbine-like product(s) with loss of two hydrogens
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Phenol coupling step generating tricyclic scaffold precursor
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms known precursor to downstream tropolone-forming step
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in N. benthamiana and heterologous expression in yeast microsomes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression; substrate feeding; yeast microsome assays; LC-MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functional in transient leaves and yeast microsomes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GsCYP75A110 consumes autumnaline and forms m/z 372.1805 products consistent with isoandrocymbine. 2. Activity was reproduced in both N. benthamiana and yeast. 3. One product is consumed by GsOMT4, supporting its role as the relevant phenol-coupled intermediate.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables oxidative phenol coupling required for progression to tropolone precursor formation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential oxidative scaffold-forming enzyme for synthetic pathway assembly
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Produces multiple isomeric peaks, indicating possible side-product formation or alternative coupling isomers
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Exact identity of all three m/z 372.1805 isomers was not fully resolved

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal precursor of colchicine with tropolone pharmacophore
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates phenol-coupled intermediate 8 to generate α-methylandrocymbine (9)
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor for tropolone ring expansion
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression; LC-MS/MS; comparison to authentic α-methylandrocymbine standard
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GsOMT4 converts 8 to α-methylandrocymbine (9). 2. Product identity was confirmed with authentic standard. 3. This establishes the immediate precursor to the ring expansion step.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of 9, the substrate for tropolone ring formation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required late methyltransferase for completing pathway to N-formyldemecolcine
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Consumption of only one m/z 372.1805 isomer helped identify the productive isoandrocymbine intermediate

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal precursor of colchicine and tropolone pharmacophore-containing medicinal alkaloid
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450 CYP71 family; non-canonical scaffold-rearranging P450
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidative ring expansion and rearrangement of 9 to form tropolone-containing N-formyldemecolcine, involving breaking and re-forming two C-C bonds
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant and remarkable scaffold-forming step
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in N. benthamiana and heterologous expression in yeast microsomes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression; substrate feeding; yeast microsome assays; LC-MS/MS comparison to authentic standard
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed with identified biosynthetic genes; found through additional transcriptome mining
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functional in transient leaves and yeast microsomes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GsCYP71FB1 converts 9 to N-formyldemecolcine (10). 2. The enzyme performs an unusual oxidative ring expansion and rearrangement that generates the colchicine tropolone scaffold. 3. This step established a near-complete biosynthetic route to a direct colchicine precursor.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly produces N-formyldemecolcine and creates the hallmark tropolone ring
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value terminal engineering enzyme for production of tropolone-containing colchicine alkaloids in heterologous platforms
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Initially identified from overlooked CYP candidate; highlighted as non-canonical P450 with unusual chemistry

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal tropolone alkaloid precursor in colchicine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates phenylalanine to enter phenylpropanoid/monolignol-like route supplying 4-HDCA
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Engineered precursor supply for colchicine alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient pathway engineering in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Module co-expression, LC-MS detection of 4-HDCA, pathway dropout analysis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-clustered with module 3 genes in transcriptome analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GsPAL was included in module 1 to build 4-HDCA from phenylalanine. 2. Module 1 produced detectable 4-HDCA in N. benthamiana. 3. Dropout analysis indicated precursor-supply genes contribute to final alkaloid production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports upstream carbon flux into 4-HDCA and final alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful precursor-supply gene for de novo synthetic pathway assembly
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Module 1 may not exactly reflect native route; some early steps may be complemented by endogenous host enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Engineering support gene, not newly discovered late colchicine-specific tailoring enzyme

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal tropolone alkaloid precursor in colchicine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates hydroxycinnamate-type substrate to CoA thioester during precursor supply pathway
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Engineered precursor supply for colchicine alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient pathway engineering in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functional context in transient leaf expression
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Gs4CL was included in module 1 for engineered 4-HDCA formation. 2. The full module produced 4-HDCA. 3. However, Gs4CL dropout did not obviously affect final product accumulation, suggesting endogenous N. benthamiana activity may compensate.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to engineered precursor supply, though not essential under tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be omitted in some heterologous systems if endogenous 4CL-like activity is sufficient
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Redundant with host activity in N. benthamiana under tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Explicitly noted as the only module 1/2 gene not appearing to affect product accumulation

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal tropolone alkaloid precursor in colchicine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces activated hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA intermediate to aldehyde in engineered 4-HDCA supply pathway
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Engineered precursor supply for colchicine alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient pathway engineering in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Module co-expression, LC-MS detection of 4-HDCA, pathway dropout analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GsCCR was part of module 1 for 4-HDCA biosynthesis. 2. Module 1 enabled 4-HDCA production in N. benthamiana. 3. Dropout analysis supported importance of most module genes for final alkaloid yield.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports production of aldehyde precursor pool for de novo pathway entry
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful precursor-supply enzyme in synthetic colchicine pathway modules
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Module 1 may not fully reflect native pathway organization

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal tropolone alkaloid precursor in colchicine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates cinnamate-derived intermediate in monolignol-like pathway supplying 4-HDCA
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Engineered precursor supply for colchicine alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient pathway engineering in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GsC4H was part of module 1 engineered to produce 4-HDCA. 2. Module 1 yielded 4-HDCA in N. benthamiana. 3. Dropout analysis supported contribution to full pathway productivity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports upstream hydroxylation required for precursor formation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful in precursor-supply cassette for de novo colchicine alkaloid engineering

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal tropolone alkaloid precursor in colchicine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: DAHPS family enzyme of shikimate pathway
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: DAHP, boosting aromatic amino acid precursor supply
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Increases flux into shikimate pathway to support aromatic amino acid-derived precursor formation for colchicine alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Engineered precursor supply for colchicine alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient pathway engineering in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functional context in transient leaf expression
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GsDAHPS was included to enhance aromatic precursor supply for 4-HDCA production. 2. In the full engineered system, most module genes including DAHPS contributed to alkaloid output. 3. It acts indirectly by supporting precursor availability.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances precursor supply for de novo colchicine alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential flux-boosting gene for improving pathway productivity in heterologous hosts
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Indirect metabolic perturbation possible but not reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as engineering aid rather than late pathway-specific enzyme
